title: Old Mines Become New Energy Sources for Businesses
subtitle: How flooded coal mines are helping companies lower their heating costs and reduce carbon emissions

summary.en: Businesses are increasingly turning to flooded, disused coal mines to provide sustainable geothermal energy for their facilities. By pumping warm water from deep underground, companies can heat large spaces without relying on traditional fossil fuels. While the process offers significant cost savings, it also presents technical and legal challenges. Recent changes to government agreements in the UK are now making it easier for other organizations to adopt this technology. This development marks a shift in how we view industrial history and modern energy needs.
summary.zh: 企業正日益轉向被水淹沒的廢棄煤礦，為其設施提供永續的地熱能源。透過抽取深層地下的溫水，企業無需依賴傳統化石燃料即可為大型空間供暖。雖然此過程能顯著節省成本，但也帶來了技術與法律上的挑戰。英國政府近期協議的變更，正讓其他組織更容易採用這項技術。此發展標誌著我們看待工業歷史與現代能源需求的方式出現了轉變。

paragraphs.en: In a creative shift toward sustainable energy, businesses are finding a surprising source of heat beneath their feet: flooded, disused coal mines. While many people think of old mines as empty holes in the ground, they are often filled with water that stays at a steady, warm temperature throughout the year. By using this natural warmth, companies are finding new ways to heat their buildings while moving away from fossil fuels.
paragraphs.zh: 在邁向永續能源的創意轉型中，企業正從腳底下的礦坑發現一種令人驚喜的熱能來源：被水淹沒的廢棄煤礦。雖然許多人認為舊礦坑只是地底下的空洞，但這些空間往往充滿了全年維持穩定溫度的水。透過利用這種天然熱能，企業找到了在擺脫化石燃料的同時，為建築物供暖的新方法。

paragraphs.en: As reported by The Guardian, Lanchester Wines in the north-east of England has been a leader in this area. For the last eight winters, the company has used heat from a flooded mine to keep its warehouses at the right temperature for storing thousands of bottles of wine. By pumping water out of the mine, extracting its heat, and using a heat pump to boost that warmth, the company has successfully kept its storage areas stable. According to Veronica Cleary, a director at Lanchester Wines, this system has helped the company cut its heating bills by roughly 35 percent.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，位於英格蘭東北部的蘭徹斯特酒業（Lanchester Wines）一直是該領域的先驅。過去八個冬天，該公司一直利用淹沒礦坑中的熱能，讓倉庫保持在適合儲存數千瓶葡萄酒的溫度。透過將礦坑中的水抽出、提取熱能，並使用熱泵提升溫度，該公司成功維持了儲存空間的恆溫。蘭徹斯特酒業的董事薇若妮卡．克里瑞（Veronica Cleary）表示，這套系統已協助公司減少了約百分之三十五的暖氣費用。

paragraphs.en: However, the path to using this energy has not been simple. The Guardian noted that while the company hoped to share its experience, the early years were difficult. Some of the holes drilled to reach the water were poorly placed, and the company faced long, complicated negotiations with the government-run Mining Remediation Authority (MRA). These legal and technical hurdles are common for businesses trying to use geothermal energy—heat that comes from the Earth—according to a report by the research organization Project InnerSpace.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，使用這種能源的過程並非一帆風順。《衛報》指出，儘管該公司希望能分享經驗，但早期的推動過程相當艱難。部分為了取水而鑽探的孔洞位置不佳，且公司還必須與政府營運的礦業整治管理局（MRA）進行漫長而複雜的談判。根據研究機構「內太空計畫」（Project InnerSpace）的報告，這些法律與技術障礙，對於試圖利用地熱能（即來自地球內部的熱能）的企業來說相當常見。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, there is positive news for the future of this technology. Lanchester Wines and the MRA have recently signed a new, improved agreement that will last until 2044. Joanne Eynon, a manager at the MRA, explained that this deal makes the process much faster and easier for the company. This new agreement is now being used as a template—a model for others to follow—to help other businesses apply for similar projects without facing the same long delays.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，這項技術的未來仍有正面消息。蘭徹斯特酒業與礦業整治管理局近期簽署了一份效期至二〇四四年的全新優化協議。礦業整治管理局的經理喬安．艾農（Joanne Eynon）解釋，這份協議讓該公司的作業流程變得更加快速且簡便。這項新協議目前正被作為範本，供其他企業參考，以協助他們申請類似專案時，不必再經歷同樣漫長的延宕。

paragraphs.en: Experts suggest that this is a major step forward. Matthew Jackson, who works at Imperial College London and helped write the Project InnerSpace report, stated that the previous rules were too strict and acted as a barrier to progress. By making the application process smoother, the government is helping more companies consider geothermal energy as a real option. This is important because, as the report notes, businesses have often felt stuck when trying to navigate the complex rules surrounding underground energy.
paragraphs.zh: 專家認為這是一項重大的進步。任職於倫敦帝國學院（Imperial College London）並協助撰寫「內太空計畫」報告的馬修．傑克森（Matthew Jackson）指出，先前的規定過於嚴苛，成為進步的阻礙。透過簡化申請流程，政府正協助更多企業將地熱能視為實際的選項。這一點至關重要，因為正如報告所言，企業在試圖處理地下能源相關的複雜法規時，往往感到束手無策。

paragraphs.en: Using mine water for heat does come with technical risks that companies must manage. Drilling into old mines can release dangerous gases, which must be carefully removed. Additionally, the minerals found in deep underground water can sometimes cause pipes and heating equipment to rust or become blocked. Despite these technical needs, the high temperatures found in mine water—sometimes reaching 40 degrees Celsius at a depth of one kilometer—make it a very attractive alternative to traditional heating methods.
paragraphs.zh: 利用礦坑水供暖確實伴隨著企業必須管理的技術風險。鑽探舊礦坑可能會釋出危險氣體，必須謹慎處理。此外，深層地下水中發現的礦物質有時會導致管線與加熱設備生鏽或阻塞。儘管有這些技術需求，礦坑水中的高溫（在深達一公里的地方，溫度有時可達攝氏四十度）仍使其成為傳統加熱方式極具吸引力的替代方案。

paragraphs.en: As more companies look for ways to lower their carbon footprint and save money, the success of these early projects is becoming a guide for the future. The ability to turn a site of past industrial activity into a source of clean, modern energy is a powerful example of how technology can solve current problems. While it is still a relatively rare practice in the UK and across Europe, the combination of better regulations and proven results suggests that more businesses may soon look underground to solve their heating needs.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著越來越多企業尋求降低碳足跡並節省成本的方法，這些早期專案的成功正成為未來的指引。將過去的工業遺址轉變為現代清潔能源來源的能力，有力地證明了科技如何解決當前的問題。雖然這在英國與歐洲各地仍屬相對罕見的做法，但法規的改善與實證結果顯示，未來可能有更多企業會轉向地底，以解決其供暖需求。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A change in direction, focus, or approach.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（移動），這裡作名詞用。文中：In a creative shift toward sustainable energy, businesses are finding a surprising source of heat beneath their feet: flooded, disused coal mines.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase or improve the power or level of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常與「推動」相關，這裡指增加熱能強度。文中：By pumping water out of the mine, extracting its heat, and using a heat pump to boost that warmth, the company has successfully kept its storage areas stable.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to move forward or make progress due to difficulties.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 卡住的、動彈不得的（引申為無法進展）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞 stick 的過去分詞，這裡形容企業在法規中受阻的狀態。文中：This is important because, as the report notes, businesses have often felt stuck when trying to navigate the complex rules surrounding underground energy.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: From coal to cabernet: the wine seller using a flooded mine to cut heating bills

previousArticles.title: Turning Old Mines into New Energy Sources
previousArticles.summaryEn: Companies are increasingly looking at old, flooded coal mines as a source of geothermal energy. By pumping warm water from underground, businesses can heat their facilities more efficiently. Lanchester Wines in the UK has successfully used this method to keep its warehouses at a steady temperature. While technical and legal challenges remain, this approach offers a way to move away from fossil fuels. Experts believe this technology could become a vital part of global efforts to reach climate goals.

quiz.question: What specific benefit has Lanchester Wines experienced as a result of using mine water for heating?

options.A: They have eliminated all of their energy costs.
options.B: They have reduced their heating expenses by approximately 35 percent.
options.C: They have successfully stored wine at a lower temperature than before.
options.D: They have completely stopped using fossil fuels in their operations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Lanchester Wines 的董事 Veronica Cleary 明確指出，這套系統幫助公司將暖氣費用降低了約 35%。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章僅提到降低費用，而非消除所有能源成本。選項 C 錯誤，文中提到是維持「合適的溫度」（right temperature）以儲存酒，而非降低溫度。選項 D 錯誤，文章提到該公司正努力「減少」（moving away from）化石燃料的使用，而非已經完全停止。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why is the new agreement between Lanchester Wines and the MRA considered a significant development?

options.A: It guarantees that the mine water will remain at 40 degrees Celsius until 2044.
options.B: It provides the company with financial compensation for the technical risks they faced.
options.C: It creates a standardized framework that simplifies the application process for future businesses.
options.D: It forces the government to take over the maintenance of all geothermal heating equipment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，新的協議被當作一個「模板」（template），旨在讓其他企業能更容易地申請類似項目，避免過去冗長的延遲。選項 A 錯誤，文中提到 40 度是礦坑水的潛力，並非協議保證的數值。選項 B 錯誤，協議是關於簡化流程，而非賠償技術風險。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提到政府將接管設備維護，僅提到法規上的改善。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'stuck' imply when referring to businesses trying to navigate complex rules?

options.A: They were unable to move forward or make progress due to bureaucratic obstacles.
options.B: They became physically trapped inside the underground mine tunnels.
options.C: They were forced to glue their equipment to the ground for stability.
options.D: They had to wait for a long time because the machinery was broken.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境下，「stuck」並非指物理上的卡住，而是指企業在面對複雜且嚴苛的法規時，感到進退兩難、無法推進計畫。選項 B 錯誤，這是對單字的字面誤解。選項 C 錯誤，與文章提到的能源技術無關。選項 D 錯誤，雖然文章提到延遲，但「stuck」在這裡形容的是對法規環境的無力感，而非機器故障。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the use of flooded coal mines for energy?

options.A: It is a dangerous practice that should be avoided due to the risk of toxic gases.
options.B: It is a highly profitable industry that has already replaced most traditional heating methods in the UK.
options.C: It is a promising, sustainable solution that, despite initial regulatory and technical hurdles, is becoming more viable.
options.D: It is a temporary solution that will only be effective until the year 2044.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體論述了將廢棄礦坑轉化為能源來源的潛力，雖然過程中面臨法規與技術挑戰（如氣體與生鏽問題），但隨著法規改善與成功案例的出現，這項技術正逐漸成為具吸引力的替代方案。選項 A 錯誤，文章將其視為解決方案而非單純的危險。選項 B 錯誤，目前仍屬「相對罕見」（relatively rare）。選項 D 錯誤，2044 年僅是協議期限，並非技術的終點。
